{"key_event":"Napoleonic collapse and Bourbon Charter","reasoning":"By 1818 France retained a strong centralized administrative, legal, and fiscal apparatus, but military defeat, regime change, constitutional budgeting, and foreign occupation had reduced state power from the Napoleonic peak. Civil society recovered somewhat through notables, censitary elections, parliamentary opposition, and protected property rights, though participation remained elite and coercion/violence still constrained broader mobilization.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":5.8,"state_change":-1.3,"state_power":7.2}
